# 

# Introduction {#introduction}

The technology industry is undergoing a fundamental shift that happens only once every few decades. We have moved past AI as a simple "copilot" for writing faster code; we have entered the Agentic Era, where the global market now demands engineers who can orchestrate entire autonomous systems. For GAPsters, this isn't just about changing how we work but ensuring that your skills remain elite and indispensable in a world where "standard" engineering is rapidly being commoditized. By raising our bar, we are collectively building a protective moat around your careers, ensuring you are the architects of the future rather than spectators.

We believe the most powerful future is one where human ingenuity is amplified by AI, not replaced by it. However, for this investment to be sustainable, we must align our growth and compensation models with this new reality. The **AI Impact Evaluation** is our commitment to you; it provides a clear, objective roadmap to mastery, ensuring that those who lead this transformation are recognized and rewarded for the immense value they bring to our clients.

Ultimately, this evolution reflects our core values: Striving for Greatness and Investing in People. Investing in people means more than just providing tools; it means providing the challenge, the coaching, and the environment necessary to reach your full potential. We aren't just raising expectations, we are moving forward together to ensure that being a "GAPster" remains the highest gold standard for professionalism and innovation in the global tech industry.

# 

# What is the AI Impact Framework? {#what-is-the-ai-impact-framework?}

We are introducing the **AI Impact Framework**, a new model designed to assess how engineers integrate AI into their daily workflows. This evaluation focuses on key behaviors that define operating in "agentic mode." The commitment to become drivers of agentic AI applies to EVERY GAPster, not just those in engineering.

# 

# Timeline {#timeline}

| Phase 1: AI Assessment |  | Phase 2: Performance Review & Calibration |  | Phase 3: Merit increases |  | Phase 4:  Progress update |  |  |
| ----- | :---- | ----- | :---- | ----- | :---- | ----- | ----- | :---- |
| **March 23rd \- April 16th** |  [**GAP AI Impact Self-Evaluation Form**](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdIWL04OKbh-uxQWNovH-VobgYfzypmzUb_PC4mLWV-h1bnmw/viewform) \+ Process Instructions | **May 11th** | Access to the Engagedly Performance Review, AI Assessment Report, and Leadership Assessment (if applicable). | **May (retroactive to April 1st)  For this cycle, merit increases will be reserved only for GAPsters who reach Level 3 (Transformer) on the AI Impact Framework.**  | Salary increases | **From June to September** |  | Monthly cycle of Progress Update to level up |
|  |  | **May 11th \- May 15th** | 1:1 Sessions: To discuss Performance Reviews, AI Assessment results, and merit increase eligibility. |  |  | **July & October** |  | Re-evaluation of Level 3 |
|  |  | **May 18th \- May 22nd** | Salary Meetings: DMs will communicate the merit increase amount. |  |  |  |  |  |

# 

# AI Support Squad {#ai-support-squad}

* **Raises & Performance Review:** Adriana Calderón and Laura Vargas  
* **Client Support:** Steven Yelton, Milagro Prado and Kenneth Rivera

* **AI Council (SMEs):** Sergio Morales, Phillipe Heymans, Daniel Ávalos, Rodrigo Rodríguez, Ana Chevez, Adriana Gómez

AI Levels Framework  
**![][image1]**

The purpose of these AI Levels is to help classify where people are in their AI journey, how AI is integrated into their daily work, and the impact it has on outcomes, so they can continue growing and progress to the next level.

## **Level 0 \- Non-Integrator** {#level-0---non-integrator}

This person does not meaningfully integrate AI into daily workflows. The use is rare or limited to very basic tasks.

**Characteristics**

* Uses AI mainly as a Google search for explanations or quick questions  
* Behavior is mostly a chatbot “copy-paster” when using it  
* The way of working remains very similar to how it was before AI  
* May show skepticism about the usefulness, reliability, or value of AI tools

**Impact Scope:** No meaningful AI impact on work outcomes.

## **Level 1 \- Operative User** {#level-1---operative-user}

This person regularly uses AI to improve personal efficiency, but the impact remains individual and is not visible at the project level.

**Characteristics**

* Uses AI for tasks such as explanations, snippets, research, documentation, or simple task assistance  
* Improves personal task speed without a clear, measurable improvement in overall project outcomes  
* Productivity gains are not translated into increased delivery, higher complexity ownership, or improved quality metrics  
* Demonstrates basic familiarity with AI tools and uses them to support individual work  
* AI is used mainly as a helper for tasks rather than integrated into the overall development workflow

**Impact Scope:** AI improves individual speed but does not change project impact or results. Work outcomes remain largely equivalent to the pre-AI baseline at the project level.

**Examples of Usage**

* Using AI to explain unfamiliar code or concepts  
* Using AI for vibe coding or basic use cases with low impact  
* Generating small code snippets or examples  
* Using AI to help draft documentation or summaries  
* Using AI for research or quick problem explanations  
* Using Gems or structured prompts for simple tasks

**Main Distinction from Level 0:** AI is used regularly to assist personal work, but the impact is limited to individual efficiency and does not affect project delivery.

## **Level 2 \- Project Accelerator** {#level-2---project-accelerator}

This person uses AI in a structured and consistent way to improve project outcomes. AI becomes an active assistant in the workflow, enabling measurable improvements in delivery.

**Characteristics**

* Regularly uses AI tools to support development, analysis, testing, documentation, or debugging  
* Integrates AI into daily workflows to complete tasks faster and increase productivity  
* Uses AI to generate, refine, and validate work outputs that impact deliverables  
* Operates with an AI-first mindset when approaching engineering tasks  
* The engineer still drives the workflow step by step while AI acts as an assistant  
* Can provide clear evidence that AI usage consistently improved project outcomes

**Impact Scope:** Project-level measurable impact. AI contributes to improvements in delivery speed, quality, or scope, but it still functions as an assistant rather than an autonomous system.

**Impact Examples**

* Increased throughput \- Deliver more work in the same time (e.g., more features, tickets, or stories per sprint)  
* Reduced cycle time or lead time \- Complete work faster from ticket start to merge or release  
* Improved quality \- Cleaner code, fewer bugs, fewer hotfixes, or less rework after QA  
* Increased client perception of delivery speed or value \- Clients perceive faster or more responsive delivery  
* Ability to handle more complex work \- Taking on more complex features, integrations, or refactors without increasing team size  
* Reduced development effort \- Completing the same work with less time spent coding, debugging, or writing tests while maintaining quality

**Main Distinction from Level 1:** Impact is visible beyond the individual and can be validated through project results, metrics, or manager observation. AI improves how the project delivers value, not just how fast the engineer works.

## **Level 3 \- Autonomous Engineer (The Transformer)** {#level-3---autonomous-engineer-(the-transformer)}

This person uses AI to fundamentally change how work is delivered by operating at an agentic level, introducing automation, agents, and AI-driven systems that execute parts of the workflow.

**Characteristics**

* Designs and builds AI agents or automated workflows  
* Uses agentic AI solutions daily as part of the development workflow  
* Automates meaningful portions of the delivery process  
* Introduces reusable AI-driven systems adopted by teams or multiple projects  
* Transforms team workflows through AI-enabled processes  
* Enables new technical capabilities for clients or the organization  
* Can demonstrate systemic impact enabled by AI automation, agents, or reusable AI systems

**Impact Scope:** Systemic impact across the team or organization. AI significantly increases delivery capacity by automating parts of the workflow. Instead of only assisting tasks, AI systems execute activities with limited supervision, while the engineer designs and orchestrates these systems.

**Examples of Impact**

* Makes the whole team faster \- Agents, tools, or automated workflows that significantly increase team productivity  
* Generates new revenue \- Enabling new AI capabilities that expand project scope or create new services for clients  
* Improves project margins through efficiency \- Delivering the same work with fewer engineering hours  
* Changes how the team builds and delivers software \- Introducing AI agents, workflows, or automation that become part of the delivery process  
* Creates reusable AI tools used by multiple projects- Internal agents, prompts, pipelines, or frameworks adopted by other teams

**Main Distinction from Level 2:** Impact becomes systemic rather than project-level. AI is not only used to accelerate work but to automate and transform how work is executed across teams or systems.

# Evaluation System ![][image2] {#evaluation-system}

The goal of this process is to understand where our engineers currently stand in terms of **AI maturity** and how AI is being integrated into their daily work.

The evaluation focuses on three dimensions:

1. **Mindset:** How you approach and incorporate AI into your workflow.  
2. **Capacity:** What you are able to do with AI in practice.  
3. **Impact:** How your use of AI improves project and delivery outcomes.

And evaluates 5 different areas:

1. **AI integration in your daily workflow**

**Objective:** Transition from a "Manual-First" to an "AI-First" workflow.

**Focus:** Habitual use of AI tools for daily planning, code scaffolding, and administrative offloading. This area measures how effectively you use the machine to handle boilerplate work so you can focus on high-level logic and shared team innovation.

2. **Appropriate use of AI and validation of AI outputs**  
   **Objective:** Maintain a "Human-in-the-Loop" architecture for safety and standards.  
   **Focus:** Critical supervision of AI outputs. This involves active verification to catch "hallucinations" (errors), refactoring code to meet organizational patterns, and ensuring no technical debt or security vulnerabilities are introduced by the AI.  
3. **Productivity and measurable impact**  
   **Objective:** Translate AI usage into tangible economic value (ROI).  
   **Focus:** Proving speed and quality gains with hard data. This measures your ability to identify manual bottlenecks and to provide metrics (such as Jira velocity or reduced PR cycles) that demonstrate how AI is maximizing revenue per engineer.  
4. **Capacity to operate at an agentic level**  
   **Objective:** Evolve from a "Tool User" to a "System Orchestrator."  
   **Focus:** The creation of autonomous assets. This evaluates your ability to build AI agents, automate CI/CD pipelines, and customize tools (like RAG or custom system prompts) to solve complex, project-specific technical challenges.  
5. **Use of AI as a multiplier to scale your impact**  
   **Objective:** Act as a "Multiplier" for the organization and its clients.  
   **Focus:** Scaling intelligence. This measures your contribution to shared prompt libraries, mentoring others through "AI anxiety," and delivering AI-driven capabilities that provide measurable strategic value or growth for our clients.

## **Step 1: Self-Evaluation and Evidence Collection** {#step-1:-self-evaluation-and-evidence-collection}

**Goal:** Each engineer will complete this [**GAP AI Impact Self-Evaluation Form**](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdIWL04OKbh-uxQWNovH-VobgYfzypmzUb_PC4mLWV-h1bnmw/viewform) to explain how AI is integrated into their daily work and what real impact it is producing. The focus should be on improvements, results, and ways of working that have been demonstrated in the **past 6 months** and are now part of your normal workflow.

Please support your answers with **real examples**, not hypothetical scenarios. The information submitted in this form will be reviewed and validated as part of the evaluation process, so responses should be based on realistic evidence and should avoid exaggerated, misleading, or unverifiable claims.

**Important:** To comply with our security policy, we cannot accept full client source code. Please share only a limited snippet or an image of the relevant section. Make sure to remove any sensitive information or credentials before sending, as we are restricted from accessing the full repository.

In the form, you will be asked to provide information and evidence across **five areas**:

1. AI integration in your daily workflow  
2. Appropriate use of AI and validation of AI outputs  
3. Productivity and measurable impact  
4. Capacity to operate at an agentic level  
5. Use of AI as a multiplier to scale your impact

## **Step 2: Multi-Agent AI Evaluation** {#step-2:-multi-agent-ai-evaluation}

**Goal:** The information collected in Step 1 will be analyzed by a **multi-agent AI evaluation system** that assesses the five evaluation categories.

More than 20 dimensions are evaluated across these categories. Each dimension is reviewed through a system of **three AI agents** working together to improve the reliability of the evaluation:

1. **Evaluator Agent:** Analyzes the submitted evidence and produces an initial score.  
2. **Adversarial Agent:** Challenges the reasoning, looking for weak claims, inconsistencies, or unsupported conclusions.  
3. **Referee Agent:** Reviews both perspectives and determines the final score for that dimension.

All dimension scores are then **aggregated into a composite result**, which produces an initial **AI Maturity classification**.

## **Step 3: Report Generation** {#step-3:-report-generation}

**Goal:** After the submission period closes, a classification agent determines the appropriate **AI maturity classification** based on the evaluation scores and generates a report for each manager summarizing the results for their team members.

The report includes the proposed maturity classification, the overall AI maturity score, a summary of the evaluation reasoning, and the key information and evidence submitted in the self-evaluation.

## **Step 4: Human-in-the-Loop Validation (Manager Review)** {#step-4:-human-in-the-loop-validation-(manager-review)}

**Goal:** Each report will be reviewed by the manager in collaboration with the corresponding project lead to validate whether the proposed evaluation accurately reflects the employee's real impact of AI usage in project work.

Managers can take one of two actions: **confirm the proposed level** or **challenge it** if they believe it does not reflect reality. They can propose an adjustment if they believe the level should be higher or lower based on their observations. When proposing a change, the manager must provide a short explanation grounded in project outcomes, delivery performance, and actual AI usage patterns.

## 

## **Step 5: Dispute and Final Calibration Review** {#step-5:-dispute-and-final-calibration-review}

**Goal:** To ensure fairness and consistency across the organization, all challenged evaluations will be reviewed and approved by the corresponding Director.

This step determines the **final AI maturity classification** for each employee. Once the review is completed, managers and personal leads will receive the finalized results, and the outcomes will be shared with the relevant departments for any next steps.

All steps of the process will be **recorded for transparency and future reference**. The results can be used to track progress across evaluation cycles, identify development opportunities, and guide future initiatives related to **AI adoption and engineering effectiveness**. These records may also inform future evaluation cycles.

# **Resource Directory** {#resource-directory}

GAP provides a comprehensive suite of tools and training to help you start or accelerate your journey toward becoming a Level 3 Transformer. Make sure to review the [Official and blacklisted applications list](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1dDYLUIiRGYaRybShnEUsC5LVamoz4TVJs3MR17VB20k/edit?gid=0#gid=0) first.

Explore the resources available to you today:

## **For Technical Roles** {#for-technical-roles}

* Explore the [AI Corner in the GAPsters Hub](https://sites.google.com/growthaccelerationpartners.com/gapsters-hub/ai-corner)  
* Anthropic Free courses  
* **Autonomous Engineer Training:** Currently assigned to *Technical Leaders and selected GAPster* from specific projects, as a practical boost to elevate agentic development. We will provide more sessions on this training soon.   
* **Gemini for All:** Enterprise access to Gemini is available for every GAPster to integrate into their daily workflows.  
* **AI License Requests:** Need access to other specialized AI licenses? Coordinate your request with Erika Carvajal or Paola Solano.   
* **GAP Academy:** Access foundational AI courses (with more advanced tracks coming soon) at [academy.wearegap.com](http://academy.wearegap.com).  
* **Platform Access:** Request access to specific development and AI platforms via the [GAPsters Hub Education Page.](https://sites.google.com/growthaccelerationpartners.com/gapsters-hub/departments/education/gap-education/platforms-requests)  
* **Trainings & Certifications:** Ready to get certified? Submit your request for AI-related training and certifications [here.](https://sites.google.com/growthaccelerationpartners.com/gapsters-hub/departments/education/gap-education/certification-training-request)

## **For Shared Services & Managers** {#for-shared-services-&-managers}

* **Autonomous Admin Training:** Join the specialized sync sessions led by Juan Diego Valencia to learn how to apply "agentic" workflows to administrative and managerial tasks.  
* **Weekly People AI Office Hours:** Have questions about AI in your specific role? Drop in for our weekly sync to get live support and brainstorming.  
* **Gemini for All:** Enterprise access to Gemini is available for every GAPster to integrate into their daily workflows.  
* **AI License Requests:** Need access to other specialized AI licenses? Coordinate your request with Erika Carvajal or Paola Solano.   
* **GAP Academy:** Access foundational AI courses (with more advanced tracks coming soon) at [academy.wearegap.com](http://academy.wearegap.com).  
* **Platform Access:** Request access to specific development and AI platforms via the [GAPsters Hub Education Page.](https://sites.google.com/growthaccelerationpartners.com/gapsters-hub/departments/education/gap-education/platforms-requests)  
* **Trainings & Certifications:** Ready to get certified? Submit your request for AI-related training and certifications [here.](https://sites.google.com/growthaccelerationpartners.com/gapsters-hub/departments/education/gap-education/certification-training-request)

# **Frequently Asked Questions** {#frequently-asked-questions}

## **Merit Cycles & Retroactive Pay** {#merit-cycles-&-retroactive-pay}

1. **How does this change the current evaluation cycle?** 

The April merit cycle has moved to May. While performance reviews proceed as usual, merit increases for this cycle are now specifically tied to reaching Level 3 in the AI Assessment.

2. **Will I lose money if I'm eligible in April because of the delay?** 

No. If you qualify for a merit increase in May, it will be paid **retroactively to April 1st**.

3. **What if I don't reach Level 3 in May?** 

You have more chances. GAP has added **July and October "makeup" cycles**. If you hit Level 3 in July or October, you will receive your increase then, and you will still be eligible for your standard April 2027 cycle.

4. **Do Remote GAPsters and Talent Hub members qualify?** 

Yes. This program applies to all GAPsters regardless of location or current project status.

## **Assessment & Leveling** {#assessment-&-leveling}

5. **Who decides my level?** 

It is a hybrid process: **Self-Evaluation \+ AI Agent Analysis \+ Human Manager Validation**. Your manager, in collaboration with your project lead, will have the final say to ensure the project context is considered.

6. **Are soft skills still important?** 

More than ever. Critical thinking, communication, and problem-solving are what differentiate a human orchestrator from a tool. Performance issues in soft skills can still disqualify someone from a raise, even if they are technically Level 3\.

7. **Is the "Transformer" (Level 3\) bar the same for everyone?** 

While the framework is universal, the definition of "impact" will be tailored for Engineering vs. Shared Services (HR, Finance, etc.) and managers.

8. **If I am a Senior reaching Level 3, am I automatically a Staff Engineer?** 

No. Level 3 is a requirement for promotion, but other seniority criteria still apply. However, if you are promoted later in the year, you would receive both the AI merit increase and a separate salary adjustment for the new role.

## **Tooling, Tokens & Client Constraints** {#tooling,-tokens-&-client-constraints}

9. **What if my client doesn't allow AI?** 

You can use internal GAP initiatives (like the AI Garage) or personal projects to illustrate the maturity level in your assessment. Contact your DM or Joyce Durst if you encounter client resistance so they can advocate for you.

10. **Should I worry about token costs?** 

No. Do not "burn" tokens wastefully, but do not let cost block your impact. GAP is working on a plan to cover these costs because the engineering time saved far outweighs the token expense.

11. **Which license should I use for learning?** 

If your client's license is restricted, request a GAP account for our available tools for your practice and internal work.

## **Training & Growth** {#training-&-growth}

12. **I wasn't chosen for the first training wave. Am I being left behind**? 

No. The first wave was a pilot. More waves are coming, and you are encouraged to use L\&D resources or free courses from Anthropic and Microsoft in the meantime.

13. **Does velocity (Story Points) matter?** 

Velocity is only one factor. Level 3 is about transformation—removing bottlenecks, improving quality, or automating repeatable workflows, among others.
